source: NvChad - Additional Software: the supporting tools ripgrep and lazygit

options: A line-oriented tool that recursively searches a directory for a regex pattern
questions.why: ripgrep recursively searches for regex patterns.

options: respects .gitignore and skips hidden files/directories and binaries
questions.why: It honors gitignore rules and skips hidden/binary files by default.

questions.q: What command checks the installed ripgrep version?
questions.why: rg --version prints the version and SIMD/AVX build info.

questions.q: In NvChad, ripgrep is needed for...
On Rocky Linux 8/9, from which repository does this guide install ripgrep?
questions.why: You enable epel-release, upgrade, then dnf install ripgrep.

options: An ncurses-style interface for performing all git operations
questions.why: LazyGit is a friendly terminal UI for git.

questions.q: Which plugin lets you use LazyGit directly inside NvChad?
questions.why: lazygit.nvim opens LazyGit in a floating window.

questions.q: Inside LazyGit, which key brings up the menu of all available commands?
questions.why: Pressing ? shows the full command menu.
